Source: Talks In Switzerland To Start With Emergency Session On Lebanon; Iran Closes Strait Of Hormuz, Blames Israel Attacks On Lebanon; Soon: Voters In Colombia Vote In Presidential Runoff Election; U.S. Maternal Mortality Crisis Drives Call For Action Now. Iran Closes Strait Of Hormuz, Blames Israeli Attacks On Lebanon; Italy PM Meloni Says Pres. Trump Made Up a Story About Her; U.S.-Iran Talks Underway in Switzerland; Storms Bring Flooding From Midwest to Gulf Coast. Aired 7-8a ET

Aired June 21, 2026 - 07:00   ET

THIS IS A RUSH TRANSCRIPT. THIS COPY MAY NOT BE IN ITS FINAL FORM AND MAY BE UPDATED.

VICTOR BLACKWELL, CNN HOST: Thanks for staying with CNN This Morning.

Talks are about to get started between the U.S. and Iran, with Vice President JD Vance leading the American delegation. We'll look at the hurdles still ahead. And New Jersey Democratic Senator Cory Booker will join us live. We'll get his take on the latest efforts to end the war with Iran, and more personal conversation about family and fatherhood.

Also in Colombia, the nation is heading into a pivotal election today. President Trump has thrown his support behind a candidate promising a tougher approach to crime and drug cartels. We'll take you inside the country wrestling with how to end decades of violence.

Plus, it's Father's Day. Happy Father's Day. And if you haven't gotten dad a gift yet, don't panic. Good news. He probably doesn't want much. We'll talk about what people are really buying.

UNIDENTIFIED MALE: This is CNN Breaking News.

BLACKWELL: All right, it's Sunday, June 21st. Thanks for joining me. I'm Victor Blackwell.

Beginning with that breaking news this morning, talks between the U.S. and Iran happening in Switzerland. Vice President JD Vance is leading the U.S. side. The Vice President, along with U.S. Special Envoy Steve Witkoff and the President's son-in-law, Jared Kushner, are meeting with Iran's delegation, Iran's top negotiator, Parliament Speaker Mohammad Ghalibaf.

A diplomatic source tells CNN the discussions will begin with this emergency session on Lebanon. That's after Israel and Hezbollah traded fire just hours after reaching a ceasefire in that conflict.

Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz, they say, yesterday. They blamed Israel's strikes on Lebanon for closing the critical waterway again. And an Iranian official says that Lebanon is the country's top priority in these talks.

CNN's International Diplomatic Editor Nic Robertson joins us now from Switzerland. The Vice President says he also wants to make some progress on the nuclear issues. What's possible today?

NIC ROBERTSON, CNN INTERNATIONAL DIPLOMATIC EDITOR: Lebanon has to be possible if things are to progress. That's the way the Iranians are framing it. Today is the first time in almost two and a half months that the Vice President will be able to look the Iranian counterparts, his Iranian counterparts in the eye.

Remember, this memorandum of understanding that has taken over two months to put in place has been done through mediators. Now you're getting the principal players in the same room in this next hour together to thrash this out. And the Iranians are saying, very simply, the first point in this 14-point memorandum of understanding that President Trump and the Iranian president signed just a few days ago, the first point includes a ceasefire in Lebanon.

So the Iranians are saying, if the President, President Trump, cannot enforce that, then what else, in essence, is the document worth? What else can he or can't he enforce? And for that point, they've taken a big diplomatic stick, if you will, into this meeting, and that is closing the Strait of Hormuz, remembering that one of the points on the memorandum of understanding was they agreed to open the Strait of Hormuz.

Now they're taking that off the table, and it does appear as if they'll use that in part as leverage. Look, coming into the meeting, we know the Vice President spoke about Secretary of State Marco Rubio making good progress in Lebanon, trying to get the ceasefire there between Hezbollah and the Israeli government. Yesterday, my sources there are telling me that's been handled pretty well.

That is a good signal for these talks, but it's not clear what the Iranians are going to want in the shape of guarantees and how much they want the fighting in Lebanon diminished. That said, the head of the International Atomic Energy Agency is also in that mountaintop resort just behind me there, super secure around there, and the reason that he is there is, of course, the nuclear issues.

The International Atomic Energy Agency is in the memorandum of understanding as supervising, overseeing some of the expected steps Iran would have to take watering down its highly enriched uranium. So you can see that scene is set to get into those very difficult and thorny issues. The question is, what progress are they going to make over Lebanon? Can the Iranians lift their blockade of the Strait of Hormuz again and let the talks do what they're supposed to do in the next 58 days and get into the detail of the nuclear issue?

[07:05:10] BLACKWELL: Yes. Nic, we were just showing some video from a few moments ago of some warm handshakes and hugs there as the two delegations were meeting. We'll see how much they can achieve today.

Nic Robertson reporting for us this morning. Thanks so much.

Looking ahead this week, primary elections are set for Tuesday in several states. In Maryland, voters will choose candidates for governor, Congress, local races. In New York, the biggest battles are for open congressional seats, where progressive candidates are taking on establishment Democrats.

In Utah, several House seats are on the ballot. South Carolina voters are heading back to the polls for a runoff for the Republican gubernatorial candidate. On Wednesday, the Great American State Fair kicks off in Washington, D.C. That marks the start of America's 250th birthday celebration. President Trump is set to headline the opening event. Then on Thursday, the fair officially opens on the National Mall.

A hearing is scheduled for President Trump's former National Security Adviser John Bolton. Earlier this month, Bolton pleaded guilty to one felony count of retaining classified national security information. He's also agreed to pay a $2,250,000 fine.

Soon, voters in Colombia will head to the polls. They're casting ballots in a presidential runoff election. Colombia has, as you know, fought a decades-long drug war. Now, voters are being asked to pick a path towards ending the violence.

CNN's Stefano Pozzebon joins us now from Colombia. Stefano, last hour, we shared your story setting up the challenges of Colombia and introducing us to the candidates. They have very different directions than President Trump has weighed in.

STEFANO POZZEBON, CNN CONTRIBUTOR: Yes, absolutely, Victor. Two very different visions for how this country can tackle the chronic and historic problems that have burdened it since independence. And much of those problems have to do, of course, with narcotrafficking and cocaine production, Colombia being the number one cocaine producer in the world.

But this election, Victor, is very closely watched up in Washington and in other capitals of the world, Washington in particular. And the reason is this man, Abelardo de la Espriella. He's standing from the right. He had an almost meteoric rise to the top of the Colombian political establishment, being almost unknown to most Colombians only a year ago.

And he really ran a MAGA-styled campaign, seeking the advice and the support of Donald Trump, but also other right-wing populist figures here in South America. Think, for example, of Javier Milei from Argentina, or Nayib Bukele, or the Bolsonaro family down in Brazil. Colombia could quickly and decisively swipe towards the right, towards the MAGA-style of the right-wing world revolution that we're seeing in so many countries around the globe. If these voters and the voters of Colombia will give de la Espriella a mandate. We are in Barranquilla, which is de la Espriella's hometown. It's also, by the way, Shakira's and Sofia Vergara's hometown. So a very -- a city that has really portrayed a lot of what it means to be Colombian all around the world. And we'll be here the whole day trying to speak with de la Espriella supporters and understand whether this person can be Colombia's next president.

He's been very closely watched in Washington. In fact, at least two senators in the last -- two Congress people in the last couple of days have shown their support for de la Espriella. Bernie Moreno, for example, who is here in Barranquilla from Ohio, and he's one of the international observers that is close to de la Espriella's campaign. But also Maria Elvira Salazar, who is from Miami and represents the Florida wing of the Republican Party that is closely invested in this election.

From the left instead is Ivan Cepeda, who is a protege of the current president, Gustavo Petro. Colombia has been Washington's strongest ally in the region for the last 50 years. Four years ago, they went to the left with Petro.

Petro clashed a lot with Donald Trump, and now we're heading to the ballot, Victor.

BLACKWELL: Yes. And that last element, the connection between the U.S. and Colombia is why it's getting so much attention from Washington.

BLACKWELL: And the ramifications will have -- will be important beyond the borders of Colombia.

Stefano Pozzebon joining us this morning. Thank you so much.

So it is Father's Day. If you didn't know, rush out, get that card, whatever gift card, whatever you need. We have two voices on maternal health, why dads could be key to saving moms and babies. We'll get into it.

BLACKWELL: So this morning we are taking a closer look at a crisis that does not get enough attention. The U.S. has the highest maternal mortality rate in the developed world, and it's actually getting worse.

Health experts say that most of these deaths are preventable, and that the impact is not the same for everyone. Black women are far more likely to die from complications. But this is not just a women's health issue, it is a family issue. And today we're talking about the role that fathers can play in keeping mothers and babies safe. So joining me now, Senator Cory Booker, Democrat of New Jersey, and Charles Johnson, a father and advocate for the -- and the founder of 4Kira4Moms, whose wife died after a routine C-section with their second child. Thank you both for being with me.

Senator, I'm going to get to that in just a moment, but I want to ask you first about what's happening in Switzerland right now. The negotiations happening between the U.S. and Iran, this emergency session on Lebanon, and the Iranians want a guarantee from the U.S. delegation that Israel will not attack inside Lebanon in this fight with Hezbollah. Is that something you believe is even realistic, that the U.S. can guarantee that another sovereign state will not attack in, they say, their defense?

SEN. CORY BOOKER (D), NEW JERSEY: Well, this is another sign of the colossal failure of Donald Trump in this deal, in which already Iran is seeing the benefits, lifting sanctions, billions of more dollars, selling their oil. And we don't even have a chance to, frankly, get anything out of this deal whatsoever up front. And to your question, we know that this is a two horrible leaders, Netanyahu and Donald Trump, two criminal men, as Donald Trump has been convicted of felonies.

They are not on the same page. They're not coordinating. This is no way to go into a negotiation. And it is another sign of how Donald Trump is really failing this moment in history.

BLACKWELL: The Iranians announced Saturday that they have shut down the Strait of Hormuz again because of those strikes in Lebanon. The President posted this on social media, "There will be no tolls in the Hormuz Strait for 60 days during the ceasefire period. And there will be no tolls after the 60-day period has expired unless they are imposed by and for the United States of America, should the deal not be completed for services rendered as the guardian angel to the countries of the Middle East for purposes of both past, present, and future reimbursement of costs."

So the President says he would essentially do what the Iranians are doing. I want you to play -- I want everyone to hear what the Secretary of State Marco Rubio said. This is May 5th.

MARCO RUBIO, SECRETARY OF STATE: We cannot live in a world -- there's a lot of time and energy been invested behind the idea that international waters are free for the free flow of goods. The stuff we make in this country and export has to go through international shipping lanes. And for us to live in a world where a country can decide now we own the international shipping lane and you have to pay us if you want to use it, that is a normal that we will never be able to accept.

BLACKWELL: Both from the same administration, what's your reaction to what the President is threatening? BOOKER: Remember, everything the President has said about this war. He said he was going to get unconditional surrender. He used to send notes to the people that were being slaughtered, the protesters, help is on its way. He said he was going to get the nuclear fissile, highly enriched material out of that country. It is still there.

He said there's going to be a regime change. Well, the regime did change, but it became more extreme, not less. I don't believe anything this President is saying about it. I read the text of that deal. It is explicit. It opens the door to Iran having the chance to get money from the Strait of Hormuz, whether it's tolls or other kind of payments.

This is a cash windfall for Iran to the tune of hundreds of billions of dollars for them to rebuild their country, to them to fund terrorist proxies, for them to invest in their ballistic missile program. And so, again, there has been few cases in our lifetime where a president started a foreign policy endeavor that has been such a colossal failure.

So this President has no credibility. He is a global embarrassment right now. And unfortunately, our enemy or adversary, Iran, is really having more power than they've ever had, especially more power than they did at the beginning of this war that should not have been launched in the first place.

BLACKWELL: All right. Now, let's talk about why the both of you are here today together, and that is maternal mortality. And Charles, April 12th, 2016, your wife goes in for a routine C-section, and it happened so quickly. I mean, on the website, you all lay out minute by minute what happened. Tell the story.

CHARLES JOHNSON IV, FOUNDER, 4KIRA4MOMS: Yes, so we went in for what we expected to be a routine scheduled C-section, Victor, with a woman that was in exceptional health, right? At our, very importantly, at our provider's recommendation. Our son was born perfectly healthy. And after that, they took us back to recovery. And that's when things took a turn for the worse.

I noticed blood in Kira's Foley catheter. I brought to the attention of the doctors at Cedars-Sinai. And what took place over the next 10 hours, 10 years later, is still hard for me to wrap my mind around. She was allowed to suffer needlessly for more than 10 hours while myself and my family begged and pled for them to just see her and value her the same way they would the people that they care about most.

And unfortunately, those cries were -- fell on deaf ears. And so what I didn't understand, I'm always transparent, and I walked into the hospital that day, the thought that she wouldn't walk out to raise her boys, it never crossed my mind. But what we understand now is that this is happening not just to our family, but far too many mothers all across our country. BLACKWELL: Yes. Senator, I want you to talk to me about this Momnibus, it's a collection of legislation to try to eliminate the disparity not only in the United States as it relates to black women and other women, but the U.S. as a developed nation and other developed nations. It is just striking that the U.S. has the worst rate, and it's getting worse.

BOOKER: Yes, it's actually really tragic that we are one of the wealthiest in the country, but we have the worst outcomes for moms and worst -- most maternal deaths. And a lot of that has to be because we are failing to focus on this problem. And unfortunately, I want to say it's getting worse. It's getting worse because we're seeing these catastrophic Medicaid cuts.

And most children, nearly all -- nearly most children are born through Medicaid. We're seeing the disappearance of doctors that it's really hurting and hitting rural areas where they're just having to drive many, many more miles. So we put together a comprehensive 14-bill plan that could really address this.

A couple of them we've already been able to get passed, but these things would include expanding access to Medicaid coverage for postpartum women, expanding access to doulas and other community services. We even have a bill that Charles has been championing that is about trying to wrap comprehensive services around moms, give community information and support to dads as well during this process.

This is an achievable process. About 85 percent of these deaths, doctors say, are preventable. We can do this, America, if we focus on the problem. America should lead the world when it comes to healthy babies being born and healthy mothers.

And I just want to say to Charles, this is a guy that has turned his unimaginable pain into an indomitable purpose. He is somebody that is making a tremendous difference through his courage. This is Father's Day. He should be home. His daughter, his wife, should be doting on him and yet he's here with us today.

And behind every one of these statistics I've been saying are real dads, are real children, are real families who are going through suffering that is entirely preventable.

BLACKWELL: Can you tell me quickly about this AI tool that's been created? Because it is Father's Day. I'm talking about maternal mortality, but fathers can use this tool to protect their family.

JOHNSON: Absolutely. And so you're absolutely right. So I want to say, first and foremost, that all of those stories like Kira's are heartbreaking. We can fix this, Victor, right? And a huge part of our theory of change, in addition to all the clinical and non-clinical factors, is the role that fathers and non-birthing parents can play.

So we developed a tool, a free app that's available for everybody, called 4Kira4Dads, right? And in addition to tools that take fathers through the entire birth continuum, helping them understand everything they need to do to be able to advocate and support that mother, it also has a web, an AI coach built in.

So we don't have to go down the, you know, that black hole looking for information and disinformation. It's evidence backed. It's available for everybody. So when there is a situation, we need to know what you need to do. We have that support that's readily available 24/7, 365.

BLACKWELL: All right. Charles Johnson, Senator Booker, thank you both.

All right, the President's $14 million reflecting pool renovation is peeling up in pieces. Who is he blaming? Not nature taking its course. How criminal charges are involved, next.

BLACKWELL: Well, just hours after signing that memorandum of understanding the Strait of Hormuz, the Iranians say is closed again. Not according to the U.S. though, who say it is open.

JAQUITTA WILLIAMS, FORMER JOURNALIST: Always a journalist.

BLACKWELL: -- former anchor, but always a journalist --

BLACKWELL: -- JaQuitta Williams, and Attorney Madeline Simmons. Welcome to you all.

ERNIE SUGGS, RACE AND CULTURE REPORTER, ATLANTA JOURNAL-CONSTITUTION: Thank you.

BLACKWELL: The U.S. can say that the Strait is open, but when those insurance companies and the companies that own those ships hear the Iranians say it's shut down, it's essentially shut down.

SUGGS: It's basically shut down. We're -- it's almost like Groundhog's Day.

SUGGS: We go through this every week. The Strait is closed by Iran, but we're saying that it's open. So 55 ships went through the Strait yesterday, I believe, on Saturday. And then Iran said, hey, we're going to close it down because we don't like the way the negotiations are going out.

So, 20 percent of all oil supplies go through the Strait of Hormuz. So every time there's a threat for it to close or even a threat, you know, that's going to affect gas prices, it's going to affect oil supplies, it's going to impact everything that goes through that. So I think that what we are seeing right now is kind of a reflection of stalled negotiations.

You know, we're going to be in Switzerland today talking about that, but we're just going to keep going on this circle of just kind of just this thing not being resolved.

WILLIAMS: And bottom line, the consumer wants to know, what are they doing with these gas prices.

WILLIAMS: That's the most important part because we're already high as it is, so to know is it open, is it closed, it's just the consumer is just getting banged over the head one hit after another.

BLACKWELL: A month ago, gas at a regular gas on average was $4.56 a gallon. That's come down this morning, it is $3.94. Still nearly a dollar more than it was before this war started, but JaQuitta, to your point, if gas prices come down, penny by penny, maybe week by week, does this conflict become less of the important primary issue for the everyday American, do you think?

WILLIAMS: No, because who's to say it'll slow down?

WILLIAMS: Bottom line, you know, there are certain families who I know have a certain way that they do their gas. When they're at half point, they already fill up. They never let it get all the way to E because you never know what the price point is going to be.

And I'm sorry, it's too many people who don't have jobs, who need every penny. And to say that, well, this -- $3.94 is still not good to me, Victor.

WILLIAMS: So, you know, we need it to be in the two areas.

BLACKWELL: That's where it was --

WILLIAMS: In the one area.

BLACKWELL: Yes, well, the one area, I don't know what we're getting back there.

WILLIAMS: But at least $2.

BLACKWELL: Yes, $2.98 was where it was back on February 28th when this all started. Madeleine, for you, the vice president is there having these talks. The president joked, joked this week, that if this goes well, he's going to take credit for it. If it goes poorly, I'm going to blame J.D. Now, that was a joke. BLACKWELL: Yes. The president has been up on his social media platform and he's focused on a few things. We'll get to a couple of them. GIORGIA MELONI, ITALIAN PRIME MINISTER (through translator): Donald Trump's statements are completely made up. I am frankly astonished. I don't know why the president of the United States behaves like this toward his allies. There is one thing he should remember. Neither I nor Italy ever beg.

BLACKWELL: The president posted again this weekend. He says now she wants to be friends now that the U.S. has defeated Iran and the Iranian military, she wants to get her numbers back up. Why is this an issue for the president considering all that's on his plate?

SUGGS: It's an issue for the president because first of all, she went against him and his criticism of the pope, obviously. So, he's upset about that because he's and all the European nations, all of our allies, our traditional allies since 1918, are not on board with this war. And he's upset with that. So, you know, he's picking on her.

And, you know, just the fact that he is going to, I don't know whether or not he said it or not, you know, she says, you know, he didn't say it, that she never begged for a photo. Even for him to bring that up as a talking point is just so childish and so juvenile that she begged for a selfie. I don't even know what that means.

WILLIAMS: He's the king of petty.

WILLIAMS: Forget president, you know. And the other thing is too, he has a way of making things up in his mind and saying them over and over to make them be true. So, probably in his mind, she begged that woman probably didn't open her mouth.

SUGGS: Even if she did ask for a photo, why would he tell the world that?

WILLIAMS: Because he's the king of petty.

BLACKWELL: All right. Well, listen, we got a lot of things to get to, including the dads out there. Happy Father's Day to everybody.

WILLIAMS: Oh, Happy Father's Day.

We've all seen the condition before. That man got there on Friday. And so, what would they have to prove? What's the legal case here?

SIMMONS: I mean, it's ridiculous. I think he's going to, you know, get that thrown out easily because there's absolutely no evidence that this man specifically did anything. Now, it's peeling and they're trying to save face because it's ridiculous to spend $14 plus million on a renovation and have it be, you know, peeling.

BLACKWELL: And you know this is not the first administration to try to fix this it has been a chronic issue but the president made this an oval office issue by bringing up the investment and the price repeatedly.

SUGGS: And it was a no big contract he brought in a guy who worked on his pool. So, the whole process from the beginning was fraught.

SIMMONS: The other point of this too is if there were really folks out there defacing this don't you think we'd have video of it? Come on.

WILLIAMS: But I will say it's good to see now that Father's Day is finally getting some love compared to Mother's Day because they're night and day when it comes to celebratory things that your kids and what women want versus what men want. SUGGS: A day of rest. We don't want --

BLACKWELL: The ties.

BLACKWELL: Listen, we're both sitting here, nobody has a tie on.

SUGGS: Post pandemic we're not wearing ties to work anymore, because the kids don't give them to us anymore.

BLACKWELL: Yes. Well, maybe that's it. All right. So, let's talk about this which I think is interesting. WILLIAMS: This America, we have a problem. Who wants --

vocabulary.en: a person who is guided and supported by an older and more experienced person
vocabulary.excerpt: Ivan Cepeda, who is a protege of the current president.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他是著名画家的门徒。

vocabulary.en: a country that has agreed to help another country
vocabulary.excerpt: Colombia has been Washington's strongest ally in the region.
vocabulary.example_cn: 两国是长期盟友。

vocabulary.en: a complex or unwelcome consequence
vocabulary.excerpt: The ramifications will be important beyond the borders of Colombia.
vocabulary.example_cn: 这一决定具有深远影响。

vocabulary.en: able to be prevented
vocabulary.excerpt: Most of these deaths are preventable.
vocabulary.example_cn: 许多疾病是可预防的。

vocabulary.en: a secondary medical condition that makes a disease more difficult to treat
vocabulary.excerpt: Black women are far more likely to die from complications.
vocabulary.example_cn: 手术可能导致并发症。

vocabulary.en: a surgical procedure to deliver a baby through an incision in the abdomen
vocabulary.excerpt: His wife died after a routine C-section.
vocabulary.example_cn: 她通过剖腹产生下了孩子。

vocabulary.en: a very great failure
vocabulary.excerpt: This is a colossal failure of Donald Trump.
vocabulary.example_cn: 该项目被视为巨大失败。

vocabulary.en: the quality of being trusted and believed in
vocabulary.excerpt: This President has no credibility.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他的可信度受到质疑。

vocabulary.en: a person or group that opposes another
vocabulary.excerpt: Our adversary, Iran, is really having more power.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他们在法庭上是对手。

vocabulary.en: a strong and unyielding aim
vocabulary.excerpt: He has turned his unimaginable pain into an indomitable purpose.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他以不屈不挠的目标激励他人。

vocabulary.en: showing excessive love or fondness
vocabulary.excerpt: His wife should be doting on him.
vocabulary.example_cn: 溺爱的父母往往宠坏孩子。

vocabulary.en: an artificial intelligence system that provides guidance or training
vocabulary.excerpt: It also has an AI coach built in.
vocabulary.example_cn: 这款应用内置了人工智能教练。

vocabulary.en: a shallow pool designed to reflect a view
vocabulary.excerpt: The President's $14 million reflecting pool renovation is peeling up.
vocabulary.example_cn: 林肯纪念堂前的倒影池是著名景点。

vocabulary.en: a person who deliberately destroys or damages property
vocabulary.excerpt: Vandals, many of them have been arrested.
vocabulary.example_cn: 破坏公物者被摄像头拍到。

vocabulary.en: to spoil the surface or appearance of something
vocabulary.excerpt: Charged with destruction and defacing government property.
vocabulary.example_cn: 涂鸦损坏了建筑物外观。

vocabulary.en: below an average or expected standard
vocabulary.excerpt: They put in subpar paint.
vocabulary.example_cn: 产品质量低于标准。

vocabulary.cn: 多巴胺网站（模拟购物网站）
vocabulary.en: a website that simulates shopping to provide a dopamine rush without actual purchase
vocabulary.excerpt: Dopamine sites allow people to browse and put things in their cart but they're not real.
vocabulary.example_cn: 多巴胺网站旨在提供购物快感而不花钱。

sentences.en: A diplomat tells CNN, the first agenda item will be an emergency session on Lebanon.
sentences.cn: 一位外交官告诉CNN，第一项议程将是关于黎巴嫩的紧急会议。
sentences.analysis: 主句为'A diplomat tells CNN'，后接省略that的宾语从句'the first agenda item will be an emergency session on Lebanon'。

sentences.en: That's after fighting broke out between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon, just hours after a truce in that conflict.
sentences.cn: 那是在以色列和真主党在黎巴嫩爆发战斗之后，而就在几小时前，该冲突刚刚达成休战。
sentences.analysis: 主句为'That's after...'，其中'after'引导时间状语从句，从句中又包含'just hours after a truce in that conflict'作为进一步的时间修饰。

sentences.en: But Iran blamed the new closure on Israeli attacks on Lebanon.
sentences.cn: 但伊朗将新的关闭归咎于以色列对黎巴嫩的袭击。
sentences.analysis: 动词'blamed'后接宾语'the new closure'，介词'on'引出归咎的对象'Israeli attacks on Lebanon'。

sentences.en: This is going to be a real test today.
sentences.cn: 今天将是一场真正的考验。
sentences.analysis: 主语'This'，系动词'is going to be'，表语'a real test'，时间状语'today'。

sentences.en: And they know President Trump is sensitive to that, he said so just a couple of days ago.
sentences.cn: 他们知道特朗普总统对此很敏感，他几天前就这么说过。
sentences.analysis: 第一个分句'And they know...'后接宾语从句'President Trump is sensitive to that'；第二个分句'he said so just a couple of days ago'为并列。

sentences.en: Point number one says that there should be a ceasefire in Lebanon.
sentences.cn: 第一点说黎巴嫩应该实现停火。
sentences.analysis: 主语'Point number one'，谓语'says'，后接that引导的宾语从句'there should be a ceasefire in Lebanon'。

sentences.en: They say if the president has to be able to deliver that and they're taking away something that they agreed to, which was keeping open or reopening, if you will, the Strait of Hormuz.
sentences.cn: 他们说，如果总统必须能够兑现这一点，而他们正在收回他们同意的东西，即保持开放或重新开放霍尔木兹海峡（如果你愿意这么说的话）。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾+条件状语从句+定语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句'They say'后接宾语从句，从句中包含if引导的条件状语从句，以及which引导的非限制性定语从句修饰'something'。

sentences.en: This will be the first time that they met face to face in more than two months.
sentences.cn: 这将是两个多月来他们首次面对面会晤。
sentences.analysis: 主句'This will be the first time'，后接that引导的定语从句修饰'time'，从句中'face to face'为方式状语。

sentences.en: It seems almost incredible in that context to think that this really is the first time they will get face to face together again.
sentences.cn: 在这种情况下，想到这真的是他们再次面对面坐在一起的第一次，似乎几乎令人难以置信。
sentences.structure: 主系表+形式主语+宾语从句
sentences.analysis: 形式主语'It'，系动词'seems'，表语'incredible'，真正主语为不定式短语'to think...'，其中包含that引导的宾语从句。

sentences.en: And it was supposed to be about implementing the 14 points, but what they're actually trying to do at the moment is rescue the memorandum of understanding.
sentences.cn: 原本应该是关于实施14点计划，但他们目前实际试图做的是挽救谅解备忘录。
sentences.analysis: 第一个分句'And it was supposed to be about implementing the 14 points'；第二个分句'but what they're actually trying to do at the moment is rescue the memorandum of understanding'，其中主语为what引导的主语从句。

sentences.en: Certainly, the failure for the talks to start on Friday, and now here we are Sunday, very quickly over the issue of Lebanon, getting into the talks, seems to indicate that behind the scenes, there's confidence that progress can actually be made.
sentences.cn: 当然，谈判未能在周五开始，而现在我们到了周日，很快解决了黎巴嫩问题，进入谈判，这似乎表明幕后有信心实际上可以取得进展。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾+主语从句+同位语从句
sentences.analysis: 主语为'the failure...and now here we are...'这一长串名词短语，谓语'seems to indicate'，后接that引导的宾语从句，其中包含that引导的同位语从句修饰'confidence'。

sentences.en: The head of the International Atomic Energy Agency, whose inspectors, as part of the memorandum of understanding, would be expected to be in Iran overseeing part of whatever is agreed on the nuclear issues there is also up at that meeting.
sentences.cn: 国际原子能机构负责人也在那次会议上，根据谅解备忘录，其检查员预计将在伊朗监督那里就核问题达成的任何协议的一部分。
sentences.analysis: 主语'The head of the International Atomic Energy Agency'，谓语'is also up at that meeting'，中间插入whose引导的非限制性定语从句修饰'Agency'，从句中包含'as part of...'介词短语和'overseeing...'现在分词短语。

sentences.en: Flight data shows the Delta crew aborted its landing and climbed back into the air to avoid the other plane.
sentences.cn: 飞行数据显示达美航空机组人员中止了着陆并重新爬升以避开另一架飞机。
sentences.analysis: 主语'Flight data'，谓语'shows'，后接省略that的宾语从句，从句中两个并列谓语'aborted'和'climbed'，不定式'to avoid'表示目的。

sentences.en: And while experts say flying is safe, this is the latest in a series of close calls raising some serious concerns.
sentences.cn: 尽管专家说飞行是安全的，但这是一系列引发严重担忧的险情中的最新一起。
sentences.analysis: While引导让步状语从句，主句为'this is the latest...'，其中'in a series of close calls'为介词短语修饰'latest'，现在分词'raising'作定语修饰'close calls'。

sentences.en: The plan called for Immigration and Customs Enforcement to open the detention center in a local 1 million square foot warehouse to hold as many as 10,000 migrants, nearly tripling the population.
sentences.cn: 该计划要求移民和海关执法局在当地一个100万平方英尺的仓库开设拘留中心，容纳多达1万名移民，几乎使人口增加两倍。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾+不定式短语+结果状语
sentences.analysis: 主语'The plan'，谓语'called for'，宾语'Immigration and Customs Enforcement'，不定式'to open...'为宾语补足语，'to hold...'为不定式表目的，'nearly tripling...'为现在分词短语作结果状语。

sentences.en: Social Circle leaders said their main concern was the potential impact to their water and sewer infrastructure, which lacks capacity the concern, they say, they tried to convey multiple times to federal officials.
sentences.cn: Social Circle的领导人表示，他们的主要担忧是对供水和下水道基础设施的潜在影响，他们说，他们曾多次试图向联邦官员传达这种担忧，但基础设施缺乏容量。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾+宾语从句+定语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句'Social Circle leaders said'后接宾语从句，从句中主语'their main concern'，系动词'was'，表语'the potential impact'，后接which引导的非限制性定语从句修饰'infrastructure'，定语从句中插入'they say'作为插入语。

sentences.en: And apparently this is not happening only here in Georgia.
sentences.cn: 显然，这不仅仅发生在佐治亚州。
sentences.analysis: 主语'this'，系动词'is not happening'，表语'only here in Georgia'。

sentences.en: What changed?
sentences.cn: 什么改变了？
sentences.analysis: 疑问词'What'作主语，谓语'changed'。

sentences.en: I just have to believe that they came to the same conclusion that what we've been trying to tell them all along that Social Circle is not the right place for this type of facility.
sentences.cn: 我只能相信他们得出了同样的结论，即我们一直试图告诉他们的，Social Circle不是这类设施的合适地点。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾+宾语从句+同位语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句'I just have to believe'后接that引导的宾语从句，从句中包含that引导的同位语从句修饰'conclusion'，同位语从句中又包含what引导的主语从句。

sentences.en: And they know that has a big economic, global impact.
sentences.cn: 他们知道那会产生巨大的经济、全球影响。
sentences.analysis: 主语'They'，谓语'know'，后接that引导的宾语从句，从句中主语'that'，谓语'has'，宾语'a big economic, global impact'。

sentences.en: The Iranians have made it clear over the last couple of hours that they really want to just focus on Lebanon and resolving what's going on in Lebanon.
sentences.cn: 伊朗人在过去几个小时里明确表示，他们真的只想专注于黎巴嫩并解决黎巴嫩正在发生的事情。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾+形式宾语+宾语从句
sentences.analysis: 主语'The Iranians'，谓语'have made'，形式宾语'it'，宾语补足语'clear'，后接that引导的宾语从句，从句中'focus on'和'resolving'并列。

sentences.en: And in that memorandum, they say that there will be a ceasefire on all fronts, including Lebanon by the U.S., Iran, and their allies.
sentences.cn: 在那份备忘录中，他们说将在所有战线实现停火，包括美国、伊朗及其盟友在黎巴嫩的战线。
sentences.analysis: 主语'they'，谓语'say'，后接that引导的宾语从句，从句中'including Lebanon by the U.S., Iran, and their allies'为介词短语修饰'fronts'。

sentences.en: And the Iranians are, for their part, going to really be focused on how the U.S. can guarantee that Israel won't continue its behavior in Lebanon.
sentences.cn: 而伊朗方面将真正专注于美国如何保证以色列不会继续其在黎巴嫩的行为。
sentences.analysis: 主语'The Iranians'，系动词'are going to be'，表语'focused on'，后接how引导的宾语从句，从句中包含that引导的宾语从句。

sentences.en: I think this is a really important point to focus on, because it's going to be critical for getting any sort of more substantial deal done.
sentences.cn: 我认为这是一个非常重要的关注点，因为它对于达成任何更实质性的协议至关重要。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾+宾语从句+原因状语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句'I think'后接省略that的宾语从句，从句中包含because引导的原因状语从句。

sentences.en: Once that's resolved, they're likely to turn to nuclear negotiations.
sentences.cn: 一旦那得到解决，他们很可能会转向核谈判。
sentences.analysis: Once引导时间状语从句，主句为'they're likely to turn to nuclear negotiations'。

sentences.en: But the Iranians have said that will come down the line, maybe they'll set a framework, but none of that can go anywhere without Lebanon being solved, Victor.
sentences.cn: 但伊朗人表示，那将在之后进行，也许他们会设定一个框架，但如果不解决黎巴嫩问题，这一切都无法推进，Victor。
sentences.analysis: 第一个分句'But the Iranians have said...'后接宾语从句；第二个分句'maybe they'll set a framework'；第三个分句'but none of that can go anywhere...'，其中'without Lebanon being solved'为介词短语作条件状语。

sentences.en: I think, in fact, part of the negotiating strategy, both on the battlefield as well as at the negotiating table of the Islamic Republic for the past few months now, has been to link up Lebanon with this ceasefire precisely so that Hezbollah can continue low-intensity attacks, continue to keep things on a slow boil, and then have Israel respond, and then have the Islamic Republic call out Israel's response and force America to put pressure on its ally, Israel, rather than on its adversaries, Hezbollah or the Islamic Republic of Iran.
sentences.cn: 我认为，事实上，过去几个月来，伊斯兰共和国在战场和谈判桌上的部分谈判策略一直是，将黎巴嫩与这次停火联系起来，以便真主党可以继续低强度攻击，继续让事态缓慢升温，然后让以色列回应，然后让伊斯兰共和国谴责以色列的回应，并迫使美国向其盟友以色列施压，而不是向其对手真主党或伊朗伊斯兰共和国施压。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾+宾语从句+目的状语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句'I think'后接省略that的宾语从句，从句主语'part of the negotiating strategy'，谓语'has been'，表语'to link up...'，后接'so that'引导的目的状语从句，从句中包含多个并列谓语。

sentences.en: And the point there about sovereignty is quite key, because while the sovereignty of Lebanon is specifically mentioned in the MOU, in the Memorandum of Understanding, the right to any state to defend itself should not be impeded or precluded by this MOU, particularly states that aren't a party to the MOU.
sentences.cn: 关于主权的那一点非常关键，因为虽然黎巴嫩的主权在谅解备忘录中特别提到，但任何国家自卫的权利不应被该备忘录阻碍或排除，特别是那些不是备忘录缔约方的国家。
sentences.structure: 主系表+原因状语从句+让步状语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句'The point...is quite key'，后接because引导的原因状语从句，从句中包含while引导的让步状语从句，主句为'the right...should not be impeded...'。

sentences.en: And to that end, you know, geopolitically, as much as, you know, Prime Minister Netanyahu tries to engage in this balancing act, in terms of rhetoric, at least alone by the Vice President and even by the presidents, it hasn't been a welcoming moment.
sentences.cn: 为此，你知道，在地缘政治上，尽管内塔尼亚胡总理试图进行这种平衡行为，但在言辞上，至少副总统甚至总统单独而言，这并不是一个受欢迎的时刻。
sentences.analysis: as much as引导让步状语从句，主句为'it hasn't been a welcoming moment'，从句中'you know'为插入语。

sentences.en: The announcement by the Iranians that they have shut down the Strait of Hormuz again, this is the lever that experts of the region and foreign policy analysts have said that Iran will use over and over now that they know it works.
sentences.cn: 伊朗人宣布他们再次关闭了霍尔木兹海峡，这是该地区专家和外交政策分析人士所说的杠杆，伊朗将反复使用，因为他们知道它有效。
sentences.structure: 同位语从句+主句+定语从句+原因状语从句
sentences.analysis: 主语'The announcement'后接that引导的同位语从句，主句为'this is the lever'，后接that引导的定语从句修饰'lever'，定语从句中包含that引导的宾语从句，宾语从句中包含now that引导的原因状语从句。

sentences.en: But right now, you have a war of the press releases.
sentences.cn: 但现在，你有一场新闻稿之战。
sentences.analysis: 主语'you'，谓语'have'，宾语'a war of the press releases'。

sentences.en: So I expect to see more, not less, of these threats as negotiations and potentially deals or conflict comes our way.
sentences.cn: 因此，我预计随着谈判以及潜在的交易或冲突的到来，这些威胁会更多，而不是更少。
sentences.analysis: 主语'I'，谓语'expect'，宾语'to see more...'，后接as引导的时间状语从句。

sentences.en: The rise in artificial intelligence has sparked this building spree across America, but that growth is not welcome everywhere more than 3,000 data centers are up and running across the U.S. with another 1,500 in development.
sentences.cn: 人工智能的兴起引发了全美的建设热潮，但这种增长并非到处受欢迎，美国有超过3000个数据中心在运行，另有1500个正在开发中。
sentences.analysis: 第一个分句'The rise...has sparked...'；第二个分句'but that growth is not welcome...'，后接'everywhere'引导的地点状语从句，从句中'with another 1,500 in development'为介词短语。

sentences.en: And in the past week, a federal regulators ordered grid operators to fast-track power to those new data centers, but we've also seen protests and pushback across the country.
sentences.cn: 在过去一周，联邦监管机构命令电网运营商加快向这些新数据中心供电，但我们也看到了全国范围内的抗议和抵制。
sentences.analysis: 第一个分句'a federal regulators ordered...'；第二个分句'but we've also seen...'。

sentences.en: A lot of communities have raised concerns about the amount of energy and water those data centers consume, and of course the noise and air pollution they produce.
sentences.cn: 许多社区对这些数据中心消耗的能源和水资源量以及它们产生的噪音和空气污染表示担忧。
sentences.analysis: 主语'A lot of communities'，谓语'have raised'，宾语'concerns'，后接介词'about'引导的短语，其中包含省略that的定语从句修饰'energy and water'和'noise and air pollution'。

sentences.en: You could -- depending on the loudness, you can still hear that but it's not a clear cut off right at 20 hertz.
sentences.cn: 你可以——取决于响度，你仍然可以听到，但并不是在20赫兹处有一个清晰的截止点。
sentences.analysis: 第一个分句'You could...you can still hear that'；第二个分句'but it's not a clear cut off...'，中间插入'depending on the loudness'。

sentences.en: But the low frequency noise is problematic because it's the noise that goes through walls.
sentences.cn: 但低频噪音是有问题的，因为它是能穿透墙壁的噪音。
sentences.structure: 主系表+原因状语从句+定语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句'The low frequency noise is problematic'，后接because引导的原因状语从句，从句中包含that引导的定语从句修饰'noise'。

sentences.en: And so, we've now seen their communities that are complaining about this, some are suing.
sentences.cn: 因此，我们现在看到他们的社区在抱怨这一点，有些正在起诉。
sentences.analysis: 第一个分句'we've now seen their communities...'，后接that引导的定语从句；第二个分句'some are suing'。

sentences.en: If I have a party and it goes a little too late and it's a little too loud and someone calls the police, the police come to my house to tell me to turn it down because I'm violating some ordinance.
sentences.cn: 如果我举办派对，时间有点太晚，声音有点太大，有人报警，警察就会来我家让我关小声音，因为我违反了某项法令。
sentences.structure: 条件状语从句+主句+原因状语从句
sentences.analysis: If引导条件状语从句，从句中包含三个并列谓语；主句为'the police come...'，后接because引导的原因状语从句。

sentences.en: Yes, I mean, I think you hit the problem right there really well, because our noise ordinances and our zoning regulations are really well suited to address that nighttime party at your house, for example.
sentences.cn: 是的，我的意思是，我认为你确实很好地指出了问题，因为我们的噪音法令和分区规划非常适合处理比如你家深夜派对之类的问题。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾+宾语从句+原因状语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句'I think'后接省略that的宾语从句，从句中包含because引导的原因状语从句。

sentences.en: But they're really not well suited to deal with a noise like data centers that is just constant and very loud and just never goes away.
sentences.cn: 但它们确实不太适合处理像数据中心这样的噪音，这种噪音持续不断、非常响亮，而且永远不会消失。
sentences.analysis: 主语'They'，系动词'are not'，表语'suited'，后接不定式'to deal with...'，其中包含that引导的定语从句修饰'noise'。

sentences.en: Yes, I mean that the problem has been that once you cite things you're chasing, you know, you're behind, you know, you've already lost.
sentences.cn: 是的，我的意思是，问题在于一旦你选址，你就在追赶，你知道，你已经落后了，你知道，你已经输了。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾+表语从句+时间状语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句'I mean'后接that引导的宾语从句，从句中主语'the problem'，系动词'has been'，后接that引导的表语从句，表语从句中包含once引导的时间状语从句。

sentences.en: And what we really need to do is we really need to cite them well to begin with and avoid these lawsuits.
sentences.cn: 我们真正需要做的是从一开始就好好选址，避免这些诉讼。
sentences.analysis: 主语为what引导的主语从句'what we really need to do'，系动词'is'，后接省略that的表语从句'we really need to cite...'。

sentences.en: And that means we need better environmental regulations, we need better zoning regulations and we better need better noise ordinance.
sentences.cn: 这意味着我们需要更好的环境法规，我们需要更好的分区规划，我们还需要更好的噪音法令。
sentences.analysis: 主语'That'，谓语'means'，后接省略that的宾语从句，从句中包含三个并列分句。

sentences.en: We need noise ordinances that can address the low frequency issue, noise ordinances that can address the problems of evening nighttime and weekend noise.
sentences.cn: 我们需要能够解决低频问题的噪音法令，能够解决晚间、夜间和周末噪音问题的噪音法令。
sentences.analysis: 主语'We'，谓语'need'，宾语'noise ordinances'，后接that引导的定语从句修饰'ordinances'，第二个'noise ordinances'为重复强调。

sentences.en: A diplomatic source tells CNN the discussions will begin with this emergency session on Lebanon.
sentences.cn: 一位外交消息人士告诉CNN，讨论将以关于黎巴嫩的紧急会议开始。
sentences.analysis: 主句为'A diplomatic source tells CNN'，后接宾语从句'the discussions will begin with this emergency session on Lebanon'。

sentences.en: That's after Israel and Hezbollah traded fire just hours after reaching a ceasefire in that conflict.
sentences.cn: 这是在以色列和真主党达成停火协议仅数小时后交火之后发生的。
sentences.structure: 主系表 + 时间状语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句为'That's after...'，其中'after'引导时间状语从句，从句中包含'just hours after reaching a ceasefire'进一步说明时间。

sentences.en: Remember, this memorandum of understanding that has taken over two months to put in place has been done through mediators.
sentences.cn: 记住，这份花了两个多月才落实的谅解备忘录是通过调解人完成的。
sentences.analysis: 主句为祈使句'Remember'，后接宾语从句，其中'that has taken over two months to put in place'是定语从句修饰'memorandum of understanding'。

sentences.en: The Iranians are saying, very simply, the first point in this 14-point memorandum of understanding that President Trump and the Iranian president signed just a few days ago, the first point includes a ceasefire in Lebanon.
sentences.cn: 伊朗人非常简单地表示，这份特朗普总统和伊朗总统几天前签署的14点谅解备忘录中的第一点包括黎巴嫩停火。
sentences.structure: 主句 + 宾语从句（含定语从句）
sentences.analysis: 主句为'The Iranians are saying'，后接宾语从句，其中'that President Trump and the Iranian president signed just a few days ago'是定语从句修饰'memorandum of understanding'。

sentences.en: So the Iranians are saying, if the President, President Trump, cannot enforce that, then what else, in essence, is the document worth?
sentences.cn: 所以伊朗人说，如果总统特朗普不能执行这一点，那么这份文件本质上还有什么价值？
sentences.structure: 主句 + 条件状语从句 + 疑问句
sentences.analysis: 主句为'the Iranians are saying'，后接宾语从句，其中包含'if'引导的条件状语从句，主句为疑问句'what else is the document worth?'。

sentences.en: And for that point, they've taken a big diplomatic stick, if you will, into this meeting, and that is closing the Strait of Hormuz, remembering that one of the points on the memorandum of understanding was they agreed to open the Strait of Hormuz.
sentences.cn: 为此，他们带了一根大外交棒（如果你愿意这么说）进入会议，那就是关闭霍尔木兹海峡，记住谅解备忘录中的一点是他们同意开放霍尔木兹海峡。
sentences.analysis: 主句为'they've taken a big diplomatic stick'，'and that is closing the Strait of Hormuz'为并列句，'remembering that...'为现在分词短语作状语。

sentences.en: The question is, what progress are they going to make over Lebanon?
sentences.cn: 问题是，他们将在黎巴嫩问题上取得什么进展？
sentences.analysis: 主句为'The question is'，后接表语从句'what progress are they going to make over Lebanon?'。

sentences.en: Can the Iranians lift their blockade of the Strait of Hormuz again and let the talks do what they're supposed to do in the next 58 days and get into the detail of the nuclear issue?
sentences.cn: 伊朗人能否再次解除对霍尔木兹海峡的封锁，让谈判在未来58天内做他们应该做的事情，并进入核问题的细节？
sentences.structure: 一般疑问句 + 并列谓语
sentences.analysis: 疑问句以'Can'开头，主语'the Iranians'后接并列谓语'lift'和'let'，'let'后接宾语'the talks'和不定式短语'do what they're supposed to do'。

sentences.en: Colombia could quickly and decisively swipe towards the right, towards the MAGA-style of the right-wing world revolution that we're seeing in so many countries around the globe.
sentences.cn: 哥伦比亚可能会迅速而果断地转向右翼，转向我们在全球许多国家看到的右翼世界革命的MAGA风格。
sentences.structure: 主谓 + 介词短语 + 定语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句为'Colombia could swipe'，后接介词短语'towards the right'和'towards the MAGA-style'，'that we're seeing...'是定语从句修饰'the MAGA-style'。

sentences.en: If these voters and the voters of Colombia will give de la Espriella a mandate, we are in Barranquilla, which is de la Espriella's hometown.
sentences.cn: 如果这些选民和哥伦比亚选民给德·拉·埃斯普里埃拉授权，我们就在巴兰基亚，那是德·拉·埃斯普里埃拉的家乡。
sentences.structure: 条件状语从句 + 主句 + 非限制性定语从句
sentences.analysis: 'If'引导条件状语从句，主句为'we are in Barranquilla'，后接非限制性定语从句'which is de la Espriella's hometown'。

sentences.en: He said he was going to get unconditional surrender, he used to send notes to the people that were being slaughtered, the protesters, help is on its way.
sentences.cn: 他说他将获得无条件投降，他过去常常给那些被屠杀的人、抗议者发送便条，说援助即将到来。
sentences.analysis: 由三个并列分句组成：'He said he was going to get unconditional surrender'、'he used to send notes to the people that were being slaughtered'（含定语从句'that were being slaughtered'）、'help is on its way'。

sentences.en: This is a cash windfall for Iran to the tune of hundreds of billions of dollars for them to rebuild their country, to them to fund terrorist proxies, for them to invest in their ballistic missile program.
sentences.cn: 这对伊朗来说是一笔高达数千亿美元的意外之财，用于重建国家、资助恐怖主义代理人、投资弹道导弹计划。
sentences.structure: 主系表 + 不定式短语
sentences.analysis: 主句为'This is a cash windfall'，后接介词短语'to the tune of...'，以及三个并列的不定式短语'to rebuild...'、'to fund...'、'to invest...'。

sentences.en: And unfortunately, our enemy or adversary, Iran, is really having more power than they've ever had, especially more power than they did at the beginning of this war that should not have been launched in the first place.
sentences.cn: 不幸的是，我们的敌人或对手伊朗确实拥有比以往任何时候都更大的权力，尤其是比这场本不应发动的战争开始时更大。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾 + 比较状语从句 + 定语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句为'Iran is having more power'，后接比较状语从句'than they've ever had'和'than they did at the beginning of this war'，'that should not have been launched'是定语从句修饰'this war'。

sentences.en: And what took place over the next 10 hours, 10 years later, is still hard for me to wrap my mind around.
sentences.cn: 接下来10小时发生的事情，10年后我仍然难以理解。
sentences.structure: 主语从句 + 系表结构
sentences.analysis: 主语从句'what took place over the next 10 hours'作主语，系动词'is'，表语为'hard for me to wrap my mind around'。

sentences.en: She was allowed to suffer needlessly for more than 10 hours while myself and my family begged and pled for them to just see her and value her the same way they would the people that they care about most.
sentences.cn: 她被允许无谓地痛苦超过10小时，而我和我的家人恳求他们只是像对待他们最关心的人一样看待她、重视她。
sentences.structure: 主句 + 时间状语从句 + 方式状语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句为'She was allowed to suffer'，'while'引导时间状语从句，从句中包含'the same way they would the people that they care about most'作为方式状语。

sentences.en: And behind every one of these statistics I've been saying are real dads, are real children, are real families who are going through suffering that is entirely preventable.
sentences.cn: 在我所说的每一个统计数字背后，都是真实的父亲、真实的孩子、真实的家庭，他们正在经历完全可以预防的痛苦。
sentences.analysis: 正常语序为'Real dads, real children, real families are behind every one of these statistics'，倒装后强调'behind every one of these statistics'，'who are going through suffering that is entirely preventable'是定语从句修饰'families'。

sentences.en: So we developed a tool, a free app that's available for everybody, called 4Kira4Dads, right?
sentences.cn: 所以我们开发了一个工具，一个免费应用程序，每个人都可以使用，叫做4Kira4Dads，对吧？
sentences.structure: 主谓宾 + 同位语 + 定语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句为'we developed a tool'，'a free app'是同位语，'that's available for everybody'是定语从句修饰'app'，'called 4Kira4Dads'是过去分词短语作定语。

sentences.en: So we don't have to go down the, you know, that black hole looking for information and disinformation.
sentences.cn: 所以我们不必陷入那个寻找信息和虚假信息的黑洞。
sentences.analysis: 主句为'we don't have to go down'，'the black hole'是宾语，'looking for information and disinformation'是现在分词短语作状语。

sentences.en: And I just want to say to Charles, this is a guy that has turned his unimaginable pain into an indomitable purpose.
sentences.cn: 我只想对查尔斯说，这是一个将难以想象的痛苦转化为不屈不挠目标的人。
sentences.structure: 主句 + 宾语从句 + 定语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句为'I just want to say to Charles'，后接宾语从句'this is a guy'，'that has turned his unimaginable pain into an indomitable purpose'是定语从句修饰'guy'。

sentences.en: And the reason that he is there is, of course, the nuclear issues.
sentences.cn: 他在那里的原因当然是核问题。
sentences.analysis: 主句为'the reason is the nuclear issues'，'that he is there'是定语从句修饰'reason'。

topics.title: 美伊核谈判与中东局势
topics.content: 美国与伊朗在瑞士举行谈判，重点讨论黎巴嫩停火和核问题。伊朗以关闭霍尔木兹海峡作为施压手段，指责以色列违反停火协议。谈判涉及多方利益，包括以色列、真主党等。
topics.keywords: 美伊谈判, 伊朗核问题, 黎巴嫩停火, 霍尔木兹海峡, 中东局势

topics.title: 航空安全与FAA调查
topics.content: 波士顿洛根机场发生两架飞机险些相撞事件，Delta航班在降落时紧急复飞以避免与起飞中的American Airlines航班相撞。FAA正在调查原因，引发对航空安全的担忧。
topics.keywords: 航空安全, 跑道入侵, FAA调查, 波士顿洛根机场

topics.title: 移民拘留中心争议
topics.content: 特朗普政府放弃将佐治亚州仓库改建为移民拘留中心的计划，该计划曾引发当地强烈反对，主要担忧基础设施容量不足。DHS同时取消其他多个类似项目。
topics.keywords: 移民拘留, ICE, 社区反对, 基础设施, 特朗普政府

topics.title: 世界杯小组赛关键日
topics.content: 世界杯小组赛进入关键阶段，多支球队争夺淘汰赛名额。荷兰大胜瑞典，日本零封突尼斯，球迷创意出行方式成为亮点。
topics.keywords: 世界杯, 小组赛, 荷兰, 日本, 球迷文化

topics.title: 洛杉矶仓库火灾与环境危机
topics.content: 洛杉矶博伊尔高地一冷藏仓库火灾持续燃烧，浓烟弥漫，市长宣布紧急状态。火灾涉及生物危害和环境污染，消防员难以控制火势。
topics.keywords: 洛杉矶火灾, 仓库火灾, 环境灾难, 紧急状态, 公共健康

topics.title: 极端高温与户外安全
topics.content: 大峡谷国家公园三名徒步者因极端高温死亡，气温超过100华氏度。公园管理部门警告避免在一天中最热时段徒步。
topics.keywords: 极端高温, 大峡谷, 徒步安全, 热射病

topics.title: 哥伦比亚总统决选与毒品战争
topics.content: 哥伦比亚举行总统决选，两位候选人分别代表强硬路线和和平谈判。地雷问题、毒品战争和古柯种植是核心议题。
topics.keywords: 哥伦比亚大选, 毒品战争, 地雷, 古柯, 和平谈判

topics.title: 数据中心噪音污染与社区抗议
topics.content: AI发展推动数据中心建设，但社区抗议噪音和环境影响。低频噪音难以隔绝，现有法规不足以应对。专家呼吁改进选址和法规。
topics.keywords: 数据中心, 噪音污染, 社区抗议, 低频噪音, 环境法规

topics.title: 美伊谈判与霍尔木兹海峡危机
topics.content: 美国与伊朗在瑞士举行会谈，副总统JD·万斯率领美国代表团。伊朗以以色列袭击黎巴嫩为由关闭霍尔木兹海峡，并以此作为谈判筹码。会谈首先讨论黎巴嫩紧急局势，随后涉及核问题。
topics.keywords: 美伊谈判, 霍尔木兹海峡, 黎巴嫩, 核问题, 副总统万斯

topics.title: 哥伦比亚总统决选
topics.content: 哥伦比亚举行总统决选，右翼候选人阿韦拉多·德·拉·埃斯普里埃拉（Abelardo de la Espriella）与左翼候选人伊万·塞佩达（Ivan Cepeda）对决。德·拉·埃斯普里埃拉获得特朗普支持，竞选风格类似MAGA。哥伦比亚长期面临毒品暴力问题。
topics.keywords: 哥伦比亚, 总统决选, 毒品战争, MAGA, 特朗普

topics.title: 美国孕产妇死亡率危机
topics.content: 美国孕产妇死亡率在发达国家中最高，且仍在上升，黑人女性风险更高。参议员科里·布克和倡导者查尔斯·约翰逊讨论通过立法（如Momnibus）和AI工具（如4Kira4Dads）来改善状况，强调父亲在孕产妇健康中的作用。
topics.keywords: 孕产妇死亡率, 种族差异, Momnibus, 父亲角色, AI工具

topics.title: 林肯纪念堂倒影池维修争议
topics.content: 耗资1400万美元翻新的林肯纪念堂倒影池出现剥落，特朗普总统指责破坏者，但一名奥运选手因触摸剥落材料被捕引发争议。质疑工程质量和合同分配。
topics.keywords: 林肯纪念堂, 倒影池, 维修争议, 破坏公物, 特朗普

topics.title: 父亲节消费趋势
topics.content: 美国零售联合会预计父亲节消费创纪录达279亿美元，平均每人227美元。最受欢迎礼物包括贺卡、服装和礼品卡，但调查显示父亲最希望与孩子共度时光。
topics.keywords: 父亲节, 消费, 礼物, 家庭时间, 零售
